label: plain_text
text:
We have been reading and evaluating simple expressions for quite
some time now. Here we started by revising the meaning of some
simple expressions and their values.
       
instead of bluntly evaluating them.
To help read and evaluate complex expressions without confusion,
We use terms and brackets.
When an expression is written as a sum of terms, changing the order
of the terms or grouping the terms does not change the value of the
and the “associative property of addition", respectively.
 To evaluate expressions within brackets, we saw that when we
remove brackets preceded by a negative sign, the terms within the
bracket change their sign.
We also learnt about the “distributive property" — multiplying a
number with an expression inside brackets is equal to the multiplying
the number with each term in the bracket.
